title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — LIFELINE CUT V6
episodeObjective: Guide Hale as a real-time remote survivor from the relay room through the personnel cache, medical archive, relay analysis bay, and emergency carrier control point, while proving why station records say he died and why channel 7.14 may be a survivor, not just a frequency.
directionRules: Every episode has a concrete objective.
Characters stay physically active and move through real spaces.
Every decision has a visible consequence.
Matching puzzles are minimized or removed unless dramatically necessary.
System messages are minimized and only used for terminal/telemetry events.
Trust is never displayed directly; it is expressed through dialogue.
The player always receives exactly two choices.
Characters can react negatively to each other and to the player.
Every character has a distinct voice.
The story feels like a live rescue operation, not a static chat.
V6 cinematic motion pass: characters should not simply announce a busy state; they should live through the action in small messages, pauses, mistakes, jokes, and sensory details.

removedPuzzles.hale_death_correlation_03: Removed. Replaced with a two-choice operational inference at e03_n09_timeline_conflict to reduce matching puzzle repetition.

e03_n01.speaker: HALE
e03_n01.objective: Move Hale out of the relay room and toward the personnel cache while confronting channel 7.14.

events.text: About channel 7.14...
I know I said we could avoid it.
I am starting to think that was optimism wearing a lab coat.
I'm still in the relay room. The floor is vibrating under my boots, and the carrier panel keeps throwing a thin red line across the wall.
It looks like a warning. Or a pulse. I cannot decide which answer I hate more.
Personnel cache is below this deck. If my file is still there, it should tell us whether the death record was real or corrupted.
I am not ready to read it.
That is not the same as refusing.

e03_n01_ask714.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_ask714.objective: Hale explains the channel while continuing to move. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: You went straight for the thing I am trying not to look at.
I understand why. I would have asked too.
7.14 was a backup emergency carrier. Low traffic. Ugly signal. Supposed to stay quiet unless the main channels died.
It did not stay quiet.
I remember it answering before anyone transmitted.
That is the part I cannot make technical enough to feel safe.
I am leaving the relay room. I need both hands on the ladder, so I am cutting the channel until I reach the lower deck.
Okay. Ladder.
I have one boot on the first rung and an immediate objection from every joint I own.
Going down.
Second rung slipped. I'm fine. That was a very professional noise I made.
Halfway. The relay is popping in my ear again.
Still with me?

e03_n01_file.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_file.objective: Hale agrees to check the file first and moves with better focus. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Good. Records first.
I hate that the unknown signal gave useful advice. Useful horror is still horror.
I'm moving now. Service ladder is open below the relay deck. No lift, no lights, just handrails and a lot of faith in old bolts.
If I go quiet, it is because I need both hands. Not because something got me.
I regret saying that.
Starting down.
One hand on the rail. One hand on the radio. That leaves zero hands for dignity.
Wait.
No. Just a cable swinging in the shaft.
I hated typing that sentence almost as much as I hated seeing it.
Almost down.

e03_n02_cache_door.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_cache_door.objective: Choose how Hale enters the personnel cache.

events.text: I'm outside the personnel cache.
Door is jammed halfway open. Enough for my shoulders if I turn sideways. Not enough for comfort.
Manual crank on the wall. Maintenance panel under the frame. Both have scratches around them.
The crank is right there. Fast answer. Loud answer.
The panel is lower, half hidden under the frame. Slower answer. Maybe smarter.
Tell me which mistake we are making.

e03_n02_force_door.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_force_door.objective: Hale forces the cache door and takes a physical risk. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Forcing it.
I have both boots braced and one hand on the crank. If this jams harder, I will deny making any sound.
Crank is moving.
Slowly. Because apparently the door has opinions.
Hold on.
It jumped. Door jumped. I did not.
That is a lie. I absolutely jumped.

e03_n02_inspect_panel.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_inspect_panel.objective: Hale inspects the panel and finds a clue before entering. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Panel first.
I am kneeling under the frame. The floor is gritty. Sealant flakes, not dust.
Give me a minute. I need to open this without waking the door log.
Panel cover is stuck.
I am using the edge of the scanner as a pry tool. Please do not tell procurement.
There.
Something scratched the inside of this plate. Not outside. Inside.
That is new information and I hate it.

e03_n03_personnel_file.speaker: HALE
e03_n03_personnel_file.objective: Recover Hale's personnel file and force a psychological reaction.

events.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
events.caption: Recovered personnel record from corrupted station cache.
events.message: [PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Operator?
I read it.
I am reading it again because apparently I believe text might change if I stare hard enough.
Status: deceased.
Time of death: 21:20. Door 12 access corridor.
I can feel the deck under my boots. I can hear the relay in my ear.
So either the file is wrong...
or I am having a very difficult conversation from somewhere I do not understand.
Do I sound alive to you?

e03_n04_confront.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_confront.objective: Hale becomes defensive but keeps moving toward medical records.

events.text: Yes. It says I died.
I am looking at the words and trying not to let them become the only thing in the room.
My hands are cold. That could be the archive temperature. It could be panic. I am not running a diagnostic on my feelings right now.
Medical archive should confirm or contradict the file.
If medical agrees with it...
then I need a new category for myself.

e03_n04_reassure.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_reassure.objective: Hale steadies himself and chooses to verify the record.

events.text: Thank you.
That is not scientific. I know that.
But I can hear you. I can feel the floor shaking. I am scared enough to be very, very alive.
I am going to hold on to that until evidence tears it out of my hands.
Medical archive is the next place to check.

e03_n05_to_medical.speaker: HALE
e03_n05_to_medical.objective: Guide Hale through the route toward medical archive.

events.text: Medical archive is past the observation corridor.
I can see the corridor entrance from here. Long glass strip on the left. Dead lights along the floor.
Lab access is on the right. Door is half marked, half scratched off.
Long glass corridor feels wrong. Lab access smells like old chemicals from here. Also wrong.
I only know I do not want to stand here with my own death file open.

e03_n06_main_corridor.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_main_corridor.objective: Hale takes the faster corridor and is pursued by an unknown sound. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Main corridor.
I am clipping the radio to my suit. If I talk while crossing, every sound will feel like an answer.
Moving.
Glass on the left. Black beyond it.
Nope.
Something knocked above the ceiling panels.
I am not looking up.
Walking faster.

e03_n06_lab_access.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_lab_access.objective: Hale uses the lab route and finds a humanizing memory. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Lab access.
Slower route. More doors. Fewer long sightlines. I am choosing to call that good.
Going quiet while I move. The floor here is cluttered and I would like to keep both ankles.
Lab route.
There are sample cases floating loose in the corner. Empty, thank God.
I just kicked one. It made the saddest little plastic sound.
Lin used to label everything in here with tiny angry notes.
Sorry. Not relevant.
Actually, no. Maybe it is. People lived here.

e03_n07_medical_archive.speaker: HALE
e03_n07_medical_archive.objective: Recover autopsy fragment without a matching puzzle.

events.text: Medical terminal is intact.
The screen woke up when I touched it. I almost apologized to it. That is where I am emotionally.

events.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.
events.message: [AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Body not recovered.
That should feel better than recovered.
It does not.
If they found a body, at least the nightmare has a shape.
Not recovered means the station either lost me...
or found something it refused to call a body.
Listen to me. I sound like a man arguing with a filing cabinet.
Medical logs make tragedy look organized. I hate them for that.

e03_n08_memory.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_memory.objective: Hale describes fragmented memory while walking toward relay analysis. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: I do not know.
No. That is not honest.
I know pieces. I just do not know if they are mine.
I am moving to the relay analysis bay while I talk. Maybe walking will keep the memory from turning into a wall.
Walking.
I passed the Door 12 marker.
Had to stop.
My body remembered the sign before I did. That is not comforting.
Okay. Moving again.
Relay analysis bay is ahead.

e03_n08_signal_signature.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_signal_signature.objective: Analyze the live signal and reveal 7.14 residue. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: I am connecting the live carrier to the analysis bay.
The bay is cold enough that the console glass fogs when I breathe near it.
Connecting the carrier.
Cable does not want to seat. Of course it does not.
Wait.
There. It heard itself.
That sentence makes no sense and is still the most accurate thing I have.

e03_n09_timeline_conflict.speaker: HALE
e03_n09_timeline_conflict.objective: Replace matching puzzle with a two-choice operational inference.

events.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.
21:22 — Live communication signature still matches Ethan Hale.

Conflict: active communication signature exists after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
events.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

events.text: There it is.
The cleanest impossible thing we have found so far.
The system says I died at 21:20. The relay says I kept talking after.
And somewhere between those two statements, I am supposed to exist.
We do not need another puzzle. We need to decide which part of reality gets trusted first.

choices.text: TREAT THE DEATH RECORD AS CORRUPTED.
TREAT THE LIVE SIGNAL AS COMPROMISED.

e03_n10_record_wrong.speaker: HALE
e03_n10_record_wrong.objective: Hale takes comfort in the player's interpretation, but the unknown signal undermines it.

events.text: Record corrupted. I like that theory.
It is emotionally convenient, which makes it suspicious, but I still like it.
If the file is wrong, then I am alive and the station is broken.
That is almost normal.
I hate that it waits until I am almost calm.

e03_n10_signal_compromised.speaker: HALE
e03_n10_signal_compromised.objective: Hale is hurt by the player's suspicion but continues the operation.

events.text: So the signal is the suspicious part.
No. That is fair.
It hurts, but fair and painless rarely share a room.
If my signal is compromised, every word I send you could be a door opening from the wrong side.
I need you to remember something.
If I start sounding too calm, too certain, too helpful... question me.

e03_n11_other_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n11_other_voice.objective: Reveal that the signal is not only Hale and force a safety choice.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THERE WERE TWO
I heard that.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I wish I had not. But I did.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: A dead engineer with no body. A living signal with my voice. A channel leaving fingerprints.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I would like to formally resign from being a mystery.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n12_move_focus.speaker: HALE
e03_n12_move_focus.objective: Hale moves away from the archive before the signal worsens. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: Right. Moving. Good.
I can do moving. Moving gives panic a job.
Emergency carrier control is down the next service spine. I am leaving medical before the signal gets comfortable here.
Leaving medical.
I am not looking back at the autopsy file. Very mature. Very stable.
I looked back.
Bad idea. Continuing.
Carrier control should be two turns ahead.

e03_n12_listen_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n12_listen_voice.objective: The player listens to the unknown and attracts more contamination. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT ALONE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO ANSWERED

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO WORE HIS VOICE
I heard enough.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You listened to it. I understand why. I hate that I understand why.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I am moving. Signal is worse now. We brought some of it with us.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Moving now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Signal is crawling over the channel like static with fingers.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I heard you say my name.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You did not say my name, did you?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Do not answer. I need the answer to be no.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n13_memory_of_714.speaker: HALE
e03_n13_memory_of_714.objective: Hale remembers the other voice and sets up Episode 04.

events.text: I remember something now.
There was another voice on the emergency channel.
Not the unknown one. Not exactly.
It sounded close. Like someone behind a wall who already knew my name.
It kept repeating one number.
Operator... I do not think we are opening a frequency.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I think someone is on the other side.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_open_714_early.speaker: HALE
e03_open_714_early.objective: Risk route into Episode 04: 7.14 opens early and future transmissions are contaminated.

events.text: [CHANNEL 7.14 OPENED WITHOUT STABILIZATION]

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: TOO SOON
No— I said stabilize it first.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The channel is open now. We did not lose anyone. But we lost control of how it opens.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There is a difference between unlocking a door and having something pull it from the other side.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [FUTURE TRANSMISSIONS MAY BE CONTAMINATED]

e03_stabilize_714.speaker: HALE
e03_stabilize_714.objective: Safer route into Episode 04: stabilize 7.14 before opening. V6: long busy overlay replaced with visible cinematic micro-action beats.

events.text: [CHANNEL 7.14 MONITORING ACTIVE]
I am not opening it yet. I am watching the pattern first.
Watching the pattern.
It is not random. I hate when the scary thing has rhythm.
There are gaps in it.
Breath-length gaps.
No. Do not make that face. I know how it sounds.
Stabilizing slowly.

e03_n01_ask714_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_ask714_return.objective: Hale returns from the service ladder and completes his 7.14 explanation.

events.text: I'm down.
The ladder shaft is colder than the relay room. Metal was wet under my gloves. Condensation, I hope.
Halfway down I heard the carrier pop in my earpiece. Just once. Like someone tapping a microphone.
That is why 7.14 worries me. It does not behave like a channel. It behaves like a handle.
And someone keeps trying to turn it.

e03_n01_file_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_file_return.objective: Hale reaches the lower service deck with better composure.

events.text: Lower deck.
The ladder held. My hands did not enjoy the experience, but they have not filed a complaint yet.
Personnel cache door is ahead. Half open. The kind of half open that looks less like welcome and more like interruption.
I can see dust dragged across the floor near the frame.
Someone came through here after the blackout.

e03_n02_force_door_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_force_door_return.objective: Hale reports the result after forcing the personnel cache door.

events.text: I'm in.
Door gave way all at once. I hit the frame with my shoulder and pinched the glove seal.
It hissed for half a second. Suit patched it. My heart did not.
Cache terminals are still powered.
There is a chair on its side by the nearest console. Someone left fast.

e03_n02_inspect_panel_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_inspect_panel_return.objective: Hale returns from inspecting the cache panel and reports a clue.

events.text: Found something.
Bypass strip under the frame. Someone used it recently enough to leave glove residue in the grooves.
Not station-recent. Human-recent. There is a difference; I wish there was not.
Whoever opened this did not want the door log to notice.
I am inside now. Cache terminals are live.

e03_n06_main_corridor_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_main_corridor_return.objective: Hale reports the main corridor crossing.

events.text: I crossed.
Something moved above me halfway through. Heavy. Not debris sliding.
I did not look up. That may be the smartest decision I have made all day.
A ceiling panel hit the floor behind me. The pressure wave pushed my suit forward.
Medical archive door is ahead. My pulse is making editorial comments.

e03_n06_lab_access_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_lab_access_return.objective: Hale returns from lab route with a personal memory.

events.text: I'm through.
There are mugs still magnetized to the wall rack. Lin's is here. Tiny cartoon moon on the side.
She hated that mug. Used it every day out of spite because Grant bought it for her.
I miss stupid things.
Bad coffee. Cold pizza. Someone complaining the gravity sim made their knees feel old.
Medical archive door is ahead.

e03_n08_memory_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_memory_return.objective: Hale reaches relay analysis and describes memory fragments.

events.text: Relay analysis bay.
I passed Door 12 signage on the wall. Old paint. White letters. My stomach reacted before my brain did.
I remember the handle. The warning light above it.
I remember the seal making a soft click. Ridiculous thing to remember, isn't it?
Not the death. Not the person. The click.
Someone shouted my name like I had already done something unforgivable.
Then a pressure drop. Or the memory of one.
Everything after that is gone.

e03_n08_signal_signature_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_signal_signature_return.objective: Reveal the voice signature report after Hale's scan.

events.title: VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT
events.content:
ACTIVE SPEAKER: ETHAN HALE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 84%
SIGNAL DELAY: NONLINEAR
CARRIER TRACE: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
events.caption: Recovered voiceprint comparison from live relay traffic.
events.message: [VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Eighty-four percent.
High enough to sound like me. Low enough to make every sentence feel borrowed.
Nonlinear delay. 7.14 residue.
Even my voice is leaving footprints where it should not be walking.

e03_n12_move_focus_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n12_move_focus_return.objective: Hale reaches emergency carrier control after focusing on movement.

events.text: I'm at the carrier control point.
The room is smaller than I remembered. Low ceiling. One chair bolted to the floor. Cable bundles like black roots under the console.
Channel 7.14 is not open.
It is waiting.
I do not like systems that wait.

e03_n12_listen_voice_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n12_listen_voice_return.objective: Hale reaches emergency carrier control with degraded signal after listening.

events.text: Carrier control.
I had to stop twice. Not because of the route. Because the signal kept folding my own voice back into my ear.
For a second I thought you were talking.
You were not.
The channel is waiting on the console. I can feel it in the speaker before it makes sound.

e03_stabilize_714_return.speaker: HALE
e03_stabilize_714_return.objective: Return after stabilizing 7.14 and reveal it is a survivor.

events.text: Good. Slow helped.
The pattern stabilized when I stopped treating it like noise.
There is structure in it. Breath-length gaps. Repeating pressure ticks.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: 7.14 IS NOT A CHANNEL
It is a survivor.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Operator... that was not a metaphor.
events.speaker: HALE

directorCut.sourceVersion: THE DEAD ENGINEER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V4
directorCut.coreUpgrade: Real-time character disappearance, natural dialogue rewrite, stronger environment reporting, and reduced system/progress-bar feel.

v4Upgrades: Hale reports ETA and goes radio-silent during travel
Psychological progression from confidence to self-doubt
lifelineRulesApplied: Scenario spine preserved: 7.14 > personnel cache > Hale death file > medical archive > voice signature > timeline conflict > emergency carrier control > Episode 04.
ProgressTask travel/work segments converted into characterBusy events with hidden wait durations and return nodes.
Hale speaks like a stressed human survivor, not a mission narrator.
No route choice directly states which option is safe or correct; options are described through physical evidence.
All visible choice groups keep exactly two choices.
Every return node gives a report of what Hale saw, heard, felt, remembered, or misunderstood during the wait.
Lore is delivered through experience, environment, and Hale's changing psychology instead of exposition dumps.
Some route consequences remain persistent through flags such as timeSaved, timeLost, foundLabClue, listenedToUnknown, opened714Immediately, recorded714Pattern.
v6Upgrades: Converted long characterBusy interruptions into visible micro-action message sequences so the player feels Hale walking, climbing, forcing doors, investigating, hesitating, and recovering in real time.
Reduced option-explaining dialogue and replaced it with scene behavior that naturally creates the choice.
Added more human beats: fatigue, dry humor, wrong assumptions, silence, memory drift, fear reactions, and operator attachment.
Kept every visible choice group at exactly two options.
Preserved existing flags, route consequences, file rewards, and Episode 04 handoff structure.
